summary_en: 5.0M shares (9.97% of shares outstanding), ¥20.0B ceiling, open-market purchase from May 15, 2026 to Feb 26, 2027. Post-completion, treasury shares exceeding 5% of issued shares to be cancelled on Mar 15, 2027. Stock split 1:5 effective Oct 1, 2026 (share count ceiling adjusts to 25.0M post-split).
title_en: Notice of Determination of Matters Relating to Acquisition of Treasury Shares and Cancellation of Treasury Shares (Acquisition under Article 165(2) of the Companies Act; Cancellation under Article 178 of the Companies Act)
translation_en_brief: Sankyu Inc. announced on May 14, 2026 that its board of directors has authorized the acquisition of its own shares and subsequent cancellation. The company will acquire up to 5.0 million shares (representing 9.97% of issued shares outstanding, excluding treasury stock) with a budgetary ceiling of ¥20.0 billion via open-market purchase on the Tokyo Stock Exchange from May 15, 2026 through February 26, 2027. The acquisition is undertaken in pursuit of the company's medium-term management plan capital policy, which emphasizes capital efficiency while achieving sustainable growth and maximum enterprise value. Upon completion of the acquisition, treasury shares held in excess of 5% of issued shares will be cancelled on March 15, 2027. The company notes that a 1:5 stock split is scheduled to take effect on October 1, 2026, after which the share acquisition ceiling will be read as 25.0 million shares. The company maintains a policy that treasury holdings should not exceed approximately 5% of issued shares, with any excess being cancelled, and that retained treasury shares are to be deployed for executive compensation and other value-creation purposes.
